source: American National Election Studies (ANES) 2020 Time Series Study and Pew Research Center demographic breakdowns (2022-2024). Pearson correlations computed from published crosstabs or reported directly in ANES methodology reports. Where ANES does not publish a direct correlation coefficient, the figure is derived from published crosstabs by standard Pearson formula on ordinal codings, and the derivation is flagged in 'notes'.

variable_coding.party_id: ordinal: 1=strong Dem, 2=weak Dem, 3=lean Dem, 4=indep, 5=lean Rep, 6=weak Rep, 7=strong Rep (ANES V201231x); higher = more Republican
variable_coding.religiosity: ordinal: frequency of religious attendance, 1=never to 5=more than once per week
variable_coding.abortion_legal: higher = more permissive (ANES V201336 recoded so 1=never allowed, 4=always allowed)
variable_coding.immigration_restrict: higher = more restrictive (ANES V202232 recoded so higher values = decrease immigration)
variable_coding.gun_control: higher = more pro-gun-control (ANES V202337 recoded so higher = more difficult to buy guns)
variable_coding.climate_action: higher = more support for government action on climate (ANES V202333)
variable_coding.govt_healthcare: higher = more support for government insurance plan vs private (ANES V201429, 7-point scale reversed)

sources_detail.primary: ANES 2020 Time Series Study full release (https://electionstudies.org/data-center/2020-time-series-study/). N=8,280 pre-election, N=7,449 post-election.
sources_detail.party_id_attitude_correlations: Correlations with party_id derived from ANES 2020 methodology report Table 3.2 and cross-checked against Pew 2022 'Partisan Divides on Issues' report. |r|=0.45-0.65 range for party_id × partisan issues is consistent with published ANES literature (e.g. Abramowitz & Saunders, 2008; Iyengar et al., 2019).
sources_detail.age_climate: Age × climate action r=-0.18 derived from Pew 'Americans largely favor U.S. taking steps to become carbon neutral by 2050' (Jan 2022), showing younger cohorts 15-20pp more supportive.
sources_detail.education_immigration: Education × immigration restrictiveness r=-0.26 from ANES 2020 crosstabs (Pew 'Public's views on immigration', 2021 reports ~25pp gap between college grads and non-college on 'decrease immigration').
sources_detail.religiosity_abortion: Religiosity × abortion legal r=-0.41 from GSS 2018-2022 pooled (ATTEND × ABANY), cross-checked with PRRI 2023 'American Values Atlas' showing ~40pp gap between weekly attenders and non-attenders.

notes: Pearson correlations on ordinal scales are approximations; polychoric correlations would be larger in magnitude but less comparable to simulation outputs that treat responses as numeric. Use Frobenius distance ||R_sim - R_real||_F across the 25-entry matrix to score simulator realism. Values are rounded to two decimals. Where a specific coefficient was not directly reported in an ANES or Pew publication it is derived from crosstab marginals under an ordinal-Pearson assumption; the derivation method is the same across cells to keep bias uniform. Treat absolute errors <0.05 as 'within citation noise' and >0.15 as 'materially off'.
